identifier: Journal of Cryptology, vol. 3, no. 2, pp. 99-111; doi:10.1007/BF00196791
summary: Introduces linked time-stamping: each new record incorporates a cryptographic hash of the previous record, forming a chain in which back-dating, forward-dating, or altering any entry invalidates every subsequent hash. This is the canonical construction for tamper-evident append-only hash-chained logs and a direct ancestor of blockchain data structures.
title: How to Time-Stamp a Digital Document
